text: Why are you ignoring me?
suggestion: Express your feelings without blame to avoid putting them on the defensive.
improved: Hey, I just wanted to check in. Is everything okay?

text: I don't know what to say.
suggestion: Show active listening and invite them to share more.
improved: That sounds interesting. I'd love to hear more.

text: Are you mad at me? I'm sorry if I did something.
suggestion: Avoid over-apologizing when nothing is confirmed. Ask direct, open questions.
improved: Hey! Just checking in to see how you're doing.

text: k
suggestion: Expand beyond single words to keep the conversation flowing naturally.
improved: Okay, that sounds good! Let me know if anything changes.

text: You need to fix this right now.
suggestion: State the problem professionally and invite collaboration.
improved: Could we look at this issue together to find a quick solution?

text: I hope I'm not bothering you, but could you maybe help me?
suggestion: Be direct and confident when asking for assistance.
improved: Whenever you have a moment, I'd appreciate your help with this task.

text: Hey. What's up.
suggestion: Add a specific topic or question to make the greeting more engaging.
improved: Hey there! How has your week been going?

text: I'm the best candidate because I need a job.
suggestion: Highlight your value and what you bring to the team, not just your personal need.
improved: I am excited about this role because my background aligns closely with the team's needs.

text: I'm sorry, I probably sound stupid.
suggestion: Practice self-compassion. Speak with ownership of your ideas.
improved: Let me rephrase that to make it clearer.

text: Please hire me, I'm begging.
suggestion: Maintain professional boundaries and emphasize mutual fit.
improved: I look forward to the possibility of contributing to your team's success.

text: I guess we can hang out if you want.
suggestion: Show clear interest instead of sounding indifferent.
improved: I'd love to hang out sometime soon! When are you free?

text: Whatever.
suggestion: Even if disappointed, maintain an open channel of communication.
improved: I understand. Let's chat more about this when we have a bit more time.

text: I am really nervous about this presentation and my hands are shaking
suggestion: Take a deep breath, slow down, and speak with steady assurance.
improved: Thank you all for being here today. I'm excited to share these updates.

text: I feel so anxious and scared that I might fail
suggestion: Slow down your pacing, pause occasionally, and breathe.
improved: This is a challenging opportunity, and I am focused on giving it my best effort.

text: We are definitely going to win this pitch! I'm so excited!
suggestion: Great enthusiasm! Maintain this high, confident energy.
improved: We have a strong proposal, and I'm very excited about our chances of success.

text: I am very confident we can deliver this project on time
suggestion: Solid confidence. Maintain strong, clear eye contact.
improved: Our team is aligned, and we are on track to deliver this project successfully.

text: This is an amazing opportunity and I love the concept
suggestion: Warm connection. Speak with inviting and positive tones.
improved: I really appreciate this opportunity and look forward to collaborating.

text: This is so frustrating! Why isn't it working?
suggestion: Remain calm, keep your tone level, and seek clarity.
improved: I am encountering a roadblock here. Let's troubleshoot this step-by-step.

text: I hate when things are delayed and I'm really annoyed
suggestion: Acknowledge the frustration calmly, then redirect to action.
improved: To get things back on track, let's look at what is causing the delay.

text: Wow! That is absolutely incredible, I had no idea!
suggestion: Acknowledge the surprise and ask a follow-up question.
improved: That is fascinating! How did you discover that?

text: I think we should look at the code structure first
suggestion: Offer your recommendation clearly and invite feedback.
improved: Let's start by reviewing the overall code structure to ensure we're aligned.

improved: It's completely valid to feel lonely. Let's start with a low-pressure step, like saying hi to someone in a hobby group or online community.

text: It's hard because I'm so left out by the world and it is starting to get to me.
suggestion: Loneliness is a signal for connection. Take it step-by-step by joining a shared-interest group or calling a family member.
improved: Feeling isolated can be really tough. Remember, you don't have to jump into deep conversations right away. Even a small connection counts.

text: Someone I considered a good friend seems to be avoiding me and I don't know if I did something wrong.
suggestion: Express your feelings using 'I' statements to avoid sounding accusatory. Give them space but check in gently.
improved: It can hurt when a friend pulls away. Try sending a gentle message: 'Hey, noticed you've been a bit quiet lately. Hope everything is okay!'

text: My friend criticized me in front of others and I feel really upset.
suggestion: Express your feelings using 'I' statements to avoid sounding accusatory. Give them space but check in gently.
improved: Try saying: 'Hey, I felt a bit left out when you guys hung out last weekend. Just wanted to share how I felt, hope we can catch up soon!'

text: I always get incredibly tense when having to make small talk and I freeze up.
suggestion: Recognize that social anxiety is common. Focus on finding one friendly face and asking a simple, open-ended question.
improved: Try shifting your attention to the environment. Remember, most people are focused on themselves, not judging you. You've got this!

text: Every time I go out, I feel tense when having to make small talk which makes me avoid parties entirely.
suggestion: Shift your focus outward onto others rather than inward on your physical symptoms. Take slow, deep breaths.
improved: It's normal to feel nervous. Try taking three deep breaths and focusing on one person. You can ask a simple question like, 'How do you know the host?'

text: I spend hours overthinking my texts before sending them and second-guessing every detail.
suggestion: Give yourself a set limit (e.g. 5 minutes) to review a conversation, then deliberately pivot to another engaging activity.
improved: Try saying to yourself: 'Most people don't analyze my words the way I do.' It's safe to let it go and focus on what you're doing right now.
